description: Addresses all Android-priority security vulnerabilities discussed in architecture review. Covers: root detection, JS bundle secret hygiene, production log stripping verification, Android App Links setup, deep link intent validation, and a security checklist for each new app built from the template. No new screens. Infrastructure and config only.

security_areas.description: Detect rooted Android devices and warn or restrict the app. expo-secure-store's EncryptedSharedPreferences is accessible on rooted devices.

behavior: Call Device.isRootedExperimentalAsync() from expo-device on cold start
If rooted: log warning via logger.warn with context
For the template: warn only — do not block. Individual apps can choose to block.
Add isRootedDevice: boolean to useUIStore so app-level code can react
Root check runs after connectivity check, before auth bootstrap in boot sequence
implementation.note: expo-device.isRootedExperimentalAsync() is heuristic-based — not 100% accurate. It is a deterrent, not a guarantee.

security_areas.description: The JS bundle in a React Native APK can be extracted and read. Any secret in the bundle is a leaked secret.
rules: EXPO_PUBLIC_* env vars are embedded in the bundle — only use for non-secret config (API URL, Pusher cluster, feature flags)
Never put API keys, signing secrets, private keys, or auth credentials in EXPO_PUBLIC_* or hardcoded in source
Secrets needed at runtime must be fetched from a backend endpoint after authentication
Pusher app key is public-facing by design — EXPO_PUBLIC_PUSHER_KEY is acceptable
Audit checklist: grep codebase for patterns: 'sk_', 'secret', 'private_key', 'password =' hardcoded strings before every release
security_areas.content: Markdown checklist Claude Code generates documenting what is and is not safe to put in env vars for this template

security_areas.description: Android logcat output is readable by other apps in debug builds. Logger must be a no-op in production for debug/info levels.

rules: debug level: only outputs in __DEV__ — verified
info level: only outputs in __DEV__ — verified
warn level: console.warn in __DEV__, remote transport in production
error level: console.error in __DEV__, remote transport in production
Production build test: run npx expo build and verify no console output in logcat for debug/info
verification.build_plugin: Add @expo/config-plugins transform to strip console.log in production via babel-plugin-transform-remove-console in babel.config.js

changes: Add env check: if process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production', include babel-plugin-transform-remove-console
This is a safety net — the logger __DEV__ gate is primary, babel plugin is secondary

security_areas.area: Android App Links vs Custom Scheme
security_areas.description: Custom URI schemes (myapp://) can be registered by any app on Android — enables deep link hijacking. App Links (https:// with verified domain) require domain ownership proof and cannot be spoofed.
security_areas.recommendation: Use App Links for all deep links in production. Custom schemes acceptable in development only.

changes: Add intentFilters in android.intentFilters for App Links
Set autoVerify: true on the intent filter
Add /.well-known/assetlinks.json to your backend domain — format documented in secrets-audit.md

expo_router_config.scheme: myapp — keep for dev/Expo Go only

security_areas.description: Validate incoming deep link URLs before processing them. Malicious links could attempt path traversal or inject unexpected routes.

behavior: Accept allowlist of valid route patterns
Reject any URL not matching allowlist
Called in (auth)/_layout.tsx before storing pendingDeepLink in UIStore

security_areas.description: Android API 28+ blocks cleartext HTTP by default. Enforce HTTPS and optionally add certificate pinning.

files_to_create.description: Android network security config. Blocks cleartext in production. Allows cleartext for local dev IP in debug builds only.
files_to_create.note: With Expo managed workflow, use expo-build-properties plugin to inject this config without ejecting

certificate_pinning.status: Not available in Expo managed workflow without bare/custom native code
certificate_pinning.recommendation: Document this limitation. Apps requiring pinning must eject to bare workflow or use a custom dev client.

security_areas.description: expo-secure-store supports requireAuthentication option which requires biometric or device PIN to read the stored value. Apply to high-sensitivity keys only.

changes: Add optional requireAuthentication parameter to get/set methods
For TOKEN_KEY: requireAuthentication: false — token must be readable without biometric (app resumes silently)
For future high-sensitivity keys (e.g. full card number, private key): requireAuthentication: true
Document which keys use which access level in a comment block at top of file

security_checklist_for_new_apps.description: Generated file that every new app cloned from this template should complete before going to production.
checklist: □ Replace EXPO_PUBLIC_API_URL with production HTTPS URL — never ship with local IP
□ Confirm no sk_, secret, private_key hardcoded values in source — run grep check
□ Configure /.well-known/assetlinks.json on your backend domain for Android App Links
□ Update app.json intentFilters with your production domain for App Links
□ Set up remote.transport.ts with your error tracking service (Sentry/Datadog)
□ Verify __DEV__ blocks strip correctly in production build — check logcat output
□ Configure EXPO_PUBLIC_PUSHER_KEY and EXPO_PUBLIC_PUSHER_CLUSTER for your Pusher account
□ Review SecureStore keys — any high-sensitivity data should use requireAuthentication: true
□ Test on non-rooted AND rooted Android device — verify root detection warning fires
□ Verify App Links work by testing https:// deep links on physical Android device
□ Confirm force update version check is wired to your backend /api/app/config
□ Confirm maintenance mode is wired and backend can toggle it
□ Run expo build (production) and verify no sensitive data in the JS bundle

files_to_create.description: useRootDetection() hook and isDeviceRooted() utility. Calls expo-device.isRootedExperimentalAsync(). Logs warning if rooted. Sets isRootedDevice in UIStore.
validateDeepLink(url, allowlist) returns boolean. Rejects URLs not matching the allowlist. Logs rejected URLs.
Pre-production security checklist for apps built from this template. Markdown format.
Documents what is safe vs unsafe to put in EXPO_PUBLIC_* env vars. Includes grep commands to audit the codebase.

changes: Add requireAuthentication option to set/get methods
Document access levels for each key in comment block
Root detection runs after connectivity check in boot sequence
Call validateDeepLink() on incoming URL before storing as pendingDeepLink
Reject and log invalid deep links
Add android.intentFilters for App Links with autoVerify: true
Add placeholder domain — documented for replacement in SECURITY_CHECKLIST.md
Add isRootedDevice: boolean field, default false

boot_sequence_update.description: Final complete boot sequence incorporating all MVPs
steps: 3. Root detection — log warning if rooted, set UIStore.isRootedDevice
4. GET /api/app/config — if 5xx: ServerErrorScreen; if maintenance: MaintenanceModeScreen; if outdated: ForceUpdateScreen
5. Theme preference applied from usePreferencesStore — before splash hides
6. Auth bootstrap — secureStore read → token expiry check → getSession or refresh → setAuth or clearAuth
9. If pendingDeepLink in UIStore: navigate to it after auth resolves

acceptance_criteria.given: App runs on a rooted Android device
acceptance_criteria.then: logger.warn fires with root detection context. isRootedDevice=true in UIStore. App continues — does not crash.

acceptance_criteria.given: A developer adds a secret API key to .env as EXPO_PUBLIC_SECRET_KEY
acceptance_criteria.then: The audit doc clearly identifies this as unsafe — EXPO_PUBLIC_ vars are in the bundle
No console.log or console.info output — babel plugin and __DEV__ gate both strip them

acceptance_criteria.given: An external app tries to intercept a deep link via custom scheme
acceptance_criteria.then: Android routes the link exclusively to this app — no interception possible

acceptance_criteria.given: A malicious deep link URL is received
acceptance_criteria.then: URL is rejected, warning logged, pendingDeepLink is NOT set

edge_cases: isRootedExperimentalAsync() may return true on some non-rooted emulators — add __DEV__ exemption so dev workflow isn't blocked
App Links require the app to be installed via Play Store or ADB with proper signing — custom scheme fallback needed for Expo Go dev testing
babel-plugin-transform-remove-console strips ALL console calls including third-party libraries — verify no critical library depends on console output
Network security config XML injection via expo-build-properties only works with eas build — local expo start uses Metro bundler which bypasses it
SecureStore requireAuthentication:true on Android requires a screen lock to be set — if device has no PIN/biometric, the call throws. Handle gracefully.
out_of_scope: Certificate pinning — requires bare workflow, documented as limitation
Jailbreak detection on iOS — Android is priority, iOS jailbreak detection added when iOS build begins
Obfuscation/minification of JS bundle — Hermes handles this in production builds
Penetration testing — template provides the controls, pen test is app-level responsibility
OWASP Mobile Top 10 full audit — template addresses the most common; full audit per app
